trivia_bullets.text: Oko's Accomplices is a Planeswalker Deck exclusive — it appears only in the 'Oko, Thief of Crowns' Planeswalker Deck (collector numbers 309-313), not in draft boosters. It shares the deck's green-blue color identity with Oko, the Trickster, the simplified Planeswalker Deck Oko version.
sources: Wizards.com: Throne of Eldraine Planeswalker Decks 2019-09-18

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: 'They function as everything from assassins to jesters in the name of their fae king.' Oko in ELD lore is a shapeshifter fey planeswalker whose spark ignited during a suppression procedure. Community and lore coverage describes Oko as playing an Oberon-adjacent role — a fae king whose followers perform whatever role he requires.
sources: MTG Wiki: Oko via WebSearch snippet
Bell of Lost Souls: The Tale of Oko 2024

trivia_bullets.text: A {2}{U} 2/3 Flying faerie creature is a solid Limited body but sees essentially no Constructed or Commander play outside of Faerie tribal. As a Planeswalker Deck card it is intentionally simple — designed to be entry-level introductory.
sources: MTGSalvation: Oko's Accomplices listing via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: Oko, Thief of Crowns (ELD-197) was banned from Standard and Brawl in November 2019, one of the fastest Standard bannings in modern MTG history. Oko's Accomplices is permanently linked to a banned planeswalker's Planeswalker Deck — an unusual position. The Deck's simplified 'Oko, the Trickster' (ELD-309) was not banned.
sources: Wizards.com November 2019 banning announcement via WebSearch snippet
